attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 140 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Arbok ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Menacing Fangs
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bye-Bye Flight
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Shuffle that Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck, and then shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

name: Pidgey

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict. If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

name: Nidoran ♀

attacks.name: Poison Horn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It uses its hard incisor teeth to crush and eat berries. The tip of a female Nidoran's horn is a bit more rounded than the tip of a male's horn.

name: Spearow

abilities.name: Evolutionary Advantage
abilities.text: If you go second, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn.

flavorText: Due to its short wings, it can't fly long distances. It wanders about restlessly and pecks at bug Pokémon.

name: Nidorino
flavorText: It's nervous and quick to act aggressively. The potency of its poison increases along with the level of adrenaline present in its body.

name: Nidorina

attacks.name: Fetch Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If the group is threatened, these Pokémon will band together to assault enemies with a chorus of ultrasonic waves.

name: Nidoking

abilities.name: Enthusiastic King
abilities.text: If you have Nidoqueen in play, ignore all Energy in the costs of attacks used by this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Venomous Impact
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Nidoking prides itself on its strength. It's forceful and spirited in battle, making use of its thick tail and diamond-crushing horn.

name: Nidoran ♂
flavorText: Small but brave, this Pokémon will hold its ground and even risk its life in battle to protect the female it's friendly with.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Moon-Viewing Invitation
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Clefairy and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its adorable behavior and cry make it highly popular. However, this cute Pokémon is rarely found.

name: Clefable

attacks.name: Follow Me
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

attacks.name: More Moon
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

flavorText: Their ears are sensitive enough to hear a pin drop from over a mile away, so they're usually found in quiet places.

name: Gloom

abilities.name: Semi-Blooming Energy
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.

name: Vileplume

abilities.name: Fully Blooming Energy
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may look at the top 8 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

name: Paras

attacks.name: Spore Ball
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

name: Dugtrio
flavorText: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Spread Filaments
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. Search your deck for a number of Grass Pokémon up to the number of heads and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The bug is mostly dead, with the mushroom on its back having become the main body. If the mushroom comes off, the bug stops moving.

name: Diglett
flavorText: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Blazing Destruction
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

name: Kakuna

abilities.name: Cocoon Cover
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

name: Charizard ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Brave Wing
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Explosive Vortex
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Caterpie

attacks.name: Leaf Munch
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Grass Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

name: Metapod

attacks.name: Defensive Posture
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can't withstand a harsh attack.

name: Weedle
flavorText: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

name: Pidgeotto
flavorText: Very protective of its sprawling territorial area, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

name: Pidgeot

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It spreads its gorgeous wings widely to intimidate enemies. It races through the skies at Mach-2 speed.

name: Raichu

abilities.name: Electrical Grounding
abilities.text: When 1 of your Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may move a Lightning Energy from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

name: Sandshrew

abilities.name: Sand Screen
abilities.text: Trainer cards in your opponent's discard pile can't be put into their deck by an effect of your opponent's Item or Supporter cards.

flavorText: It burrows into the ground to create its nest. If hard stones impede its tunneling, it uses its sharp claws to shatter them and then carries on digging.

name: Sandslash

attacks.name: Rumble
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Spike Rend
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It climbs trees by hooking on with its sharp claws. Sandslash shares the berries it gathers, dropping them down to Sandshrew waiting below the tree.

name: Nidoqueen

attacks.name: Queen Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: It pacifies offspring by placing them in the gaps between the spines on its back. The spines will never secrete poison while young are present.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Stompy Stomp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

name: Wigglytuff ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Expanding Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it gets +100 HP.

attacks.name: Friend Tackle
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

name: Zubat

abilities.name: Revealing Echo
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may have your opponent reveal their hand.

flavorText: Zubat live in caves, down where the sun's light won't reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.

name: Oddish
flavorText: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

name: Venomoth

attacks.name: Perplexing Powder
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: The wings are covered with dustlike scales. Every time it flaps its wings, it looses highly toxic dust.

name: Beedrill

attacks.name: Nadir Needle
attacks.text: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack does 120 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Second Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its whiskers are essential for maintaining its balance. No matter how friendly you are, it will get angry and bite you if you touch its whiskers.

name: Fearow

attacks.name: Beak Catch
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Carrying food through Fearow's territory is dangerous. It will snatch the food away from you in a flash!

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: As each tail grows, its fur becomes more lustrous. When held, it feels slightly warm.

name: Ninetales ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heat Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Mirrored Flames
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 140 more damage.

name: Golbat

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.

name: Venonat
flavorText: Poison oozes from all over its body. It catches small bug Pokémon at night that are attracted by light.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Come Here Right Meow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

flavorText: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

name: Persian

abilities.name: Rocket Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Giovanni's Charisma card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Torrid Torrent
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamite Fang
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Overthink
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever they flip a coin, treat it as tails.

flavorText: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: In rivers with fast-flowing water, this Pokémon will cling to a rock by using its thick lips, which act like a suction cup.

name: Poliwhirl

attacks.name: Frog Hop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon's sweat is a slimy mucus. When captured, Poliwhirl can slither from its enemies' grasp and escape.

name: Victreebel

attacks.name: Slow-Acting Acid
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, put 12 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Once ingested into this Pokémon's body, even the hardest object will melt into nothing.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Mach Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd's pace and direction of travel.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Junk Magnet
attacks.text: Put up to 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

name: Dodrio

abilities.name: Zooming Draw
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 1 damage counter on this Pokémon. If you do, draw a card.

attacks.name: Ballistic Beak
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.

name: Gastly
flavorText: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

name: Gengar

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

attacks.name: Hollow Dive
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey's shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Thumpalanche
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage for each Pokémon with a Retreat Cost of exactly 4 that you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Vaporize
attacks.text: Discard a Water Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

name: Kadabra

attacks.name: Teleportation Attack
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon's telekinesis is immensely powerful. To prepare for evolution, Kadabra stores up psychic energy in the star on its forehead.

name: Machop

attacks.name: Mountain Mashing
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Always brimming with power, it passes time by lifting boulders. Doing so makes it even stronger.

name: Bellsprout

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant.

name: Tentacool
flavorText: This Pokémon is mostly made of water. A Tentacool out in the ocean is very hard to spot, because its body blends in with the sea.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Poisonous Whip
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Tentacular Panic
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If the first flip is tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its 80 tentacles can stretch and shrink freely. Tentacruel ensnares prey in a net of spread-out tentacles, delivering venomous stings to its catch.

name: Golem ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dynamic Roll
attacks.text: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rock Blaster
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Ponyta

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: If you've been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Sea Bathing
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.

flavorText: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

name: Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Package Deal
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

flavorText: They use a plant stalk as a weapon, but not all of them use it in the same way. Several distinct styles of stalk fighting have been observed.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

name: Abra
flavorText: Abra can teleport in its sleep. Apparently the more deeply Abra sleeps, the farther its teleportations go.

name: Machoke

attacks.name: Mountain Ramming
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Its formidable body never gets tired. It helps people by doing work such as the moving of heavy goods.

name: Machamp

abilities.name: Guts
abilities.text: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

attacks.name: Mountain Chopping
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: With four arms that react more quickly than it can think, it can execute many punches at once.

name: Weepinbell
flavorText: It spits out Poison Powder to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of Acid.

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Rock Cannon
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Big Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

name: Seel
flavorText: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don't bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

name: Grimer

attacks.name: Gummy Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

flavorText: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

name: Haunter

abilities.name: Spirit Return
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your opponent's discard pile into their hand.

flavorText: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

name: Golduck

attacks.name: Aquatic Rescue
attacks.text: Put up to 4 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: When it swims at full speed using its long, webbed limbs, its forehead somehow begins to glow.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Rant and Rave
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Raging Smash
attacks.text: If this Pokémon isn't Confused, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Heroic Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: Poliwrath is skilled at both swimming and martial arts. It uses its well-trained arms to dish out powerful punches.

name: Alakazam ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dimensional Hand
attacks.text: This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is on the Bench.

name: Geodude

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It uses both hands to climb precipitous cliffs. People who see it in action have been known to take up bouldering.

name: Slowbro

attacks.name: Big Yawn
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.

attacks.name: Laid-Back Tackle
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved during this turn, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.

name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Dual Splash
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It sunbathes on the beach after meals. The rise in its body temperature helps its digestion.

name: Muk

attacks.name: Sticky Jail
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

flavorText: It's thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

name: Shellder

attacks.name: Shell Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond. Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

name: Cloyster

attacks.name: Protect Charge
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 80 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

name: Hypno

abilities.name: Here for Hypnosis
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Asleep.

flavorText: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves, such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

name: Cubone

abilities.name: Cheering Bone
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, attacks used by your Marowak do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hit Twice
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon wears the skull of its deceased mother. Sometimes Cubone's dreams make it cry, but each tear Cubone sheds makes it stronger.

name: Marowak

attacks.name: Bone Throw
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: When this Pokémon evolved, the skull of its mother fused to it. Marowak's temperament also turned vicious at the same time.

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Twister Kick
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.

name: Hitmonchan

abilities.name: Counterattack
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Excited Punch
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Excited Punch attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Charismatic Drill
attacks.text: If you played Giovanni's Charisma from your hand during this turn, this attack does 140 more damage.

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Kangaskhan ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Incessant Punching
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Blinding Ink
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips 2 coins. If either of them is tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: Seadra's mouth is slender, but its suction power is strong. In an instant, Seadra can suck in food that's larger than the opening of its mouth.

name: Seaking

attacks.name: Swim Freely
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Horn
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its horn, it bores holes in riverbed boulders, making nests to prevent its eggs from washing away.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Fish Pokémon nibble at it, but Staryu isn't bothered. Its body regenerates quickly, even if part of it is completely torn off.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Flare Combo
attacks.text: If Electabuzz is on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: These Pokémon's bodies are constantly burning. Magmar are feared as one of the causes behind fires.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Gather the Crew
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

name: Flareon

attacks.name: Destructive Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Blaze
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Inhaled air is carried to its flame sac, heated, and exhaled as fire that reaches over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Omanyte

attacks.name: Tentacular Return
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a member of an ancient, extinct species. Omanyte paddles through water with its 10 tentacles, looking like it's just drifting along.

name: Omastar

abilities.name: Primordial Tentacles
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent's Active Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Aqua Split
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Omastar's sharp fangs could crush rock, but the Pokémon can attack only the prey that come within reach of its tentacles.

name: Aerodactyl

attacks.name: Devolution Ray
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Aerodactyl's sawlike fangs can shred skin to tatters—even the skin of Steel-type Pokémon.

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Voraciousness
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put up to 2 Leftovers cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Thudding Press
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon's stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

name: Voltorb

attacks.name: Tumbling Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Suspicious Gas
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its body is full of poisonous gas. It floats into garbage dumps, seeking out the fumes of raw, rotting trash.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can remember only one thing at a time. Once it starts rushing, it forgets why it started.

name: Horsea
flavorText: They swim with dance-like motions and cause whirlpools to form. Horsea compete to see which of them can generate the biggest whirlpool.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Reckless Throw
attacks.text: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon clamps its pincers down on its prey and then either splits the prey in half or flings it away.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Splashy Splash
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw a card.

flavorText: An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

name: Gyarados

abilities.name: Untamed One
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you must discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.

name: Kabuto

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: While some say this species has gone extinct, Kabuto sightings are apparently fairly common in some places.

name: Kabutops

abilities.name: Ancient Way
abilities.text: Apply Weakness for your opponent's Active Pokémon as ×4 instead.

attacks.name: Draining Blade
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The cause behind the extinction of this species is unknown. Kabutops were aggressive Pokémon that inhabited warm seas.

name: Articuno

abilities.name: Ice Float
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Water Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This Pokémon can control ice at will. Articuno is said to live in snowy mountains riddled with permafrost.

name: Drowzee
flavorText: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children's are much tastier.

name: Kingler

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Its oversized claw is very powerful, but when it's not in battle, the claw just gets in the way.

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Bang Boom Chain
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may discard any number of Pokémon Tools from your Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When they work together, Exeggutor's three heads can put out powerful psychic energy. Cloudy days make this Pokémon sluggish.

name: Chansey

abilities.name: Lucky Bonus
abilities.text: If you took this Pokémon as a face-down Prize card during your turn and your Bench isn't full, before you put it into your hand, you may put it onto your Bench. If you put this Pokémon onto your Bench in this way, flip a coin. If heads, take 1 more Prize card.

flavorText: This kindly Pokémon lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Tactful Tangling
attacks.text: If you played Erika's Invitation from your hand during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon's true appearance remains a mystery.

name: Goldeen

attacks.name: Triple Strike
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its dorsal and pectoral fins are strongly developed like muscles. It can swim at a speed of five knots.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Mimic Barrier
abilities.text: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psypower
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It's known for its top-notch pantomime skills. It protects itself from all sorts of attacks by emitting auras from its fingers to create walls.

name: Jynx ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heart-Stopping Kiss
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, it is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Electabuzz

attacks.name: Electro Combo
attacks.text: If Magmar is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: With the coming of a storm, many of these Pokémon will gather under tall trees and sit there waiting for lightning to strike.

name: Jolteon

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Fighting Lightning
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It concentrates the weak electric charges emitted by its cells and launches wicked lightning bolts.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Conversion 4
attacks.text: Choose Grass, Fire, Water, Lightning, Psychic, Fighting, Darkness, Metal, or Dragon type. Until the Defending Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, its Weakness is now that type. (The amount of Weakness doesn't change.)

flavorText: In recent years, this species has been very helpful in cyberspace. These Pokémon will go around checking to make sure no suspicious data exists.

name: Krabby

attacks.name: Salt Water
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Ball Roll
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: These Pokémon get nervous when they're not in a group of six. The minute even one member of the group goes missing, Exeggcute become cowardly.

name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Tongue-Tied
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Bug Pokémon are Lickitung's main food source. This Pokémon paralyzes its prey with a lick from its long tongue, then swallows the prey whole.

name: Weezing

abilities.name: Let's Have a Blast
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Spinning Fumes
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Very rarely, a sudden mutation can result in two small Koffing twins becoming conjoined as a Weezing.

name: Starmie

abilities.name: Mysterious Comet
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards.

flavorText: Starmie swims by spinning its body at high speed. As this Pokémon cruises through the ocean, it absorbs tiny plankton.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Helpful Slash
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Grass Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Hop on My Back
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Transformative Start
abilities.text: Once during your first turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may search your deck and choose a Basic Pokémon you find there, except any Ditto. If you do, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards, and put the chosen Pokémon in its place. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can't maintain its disguise.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Colorful Friends
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon of different types, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.

name: Vaporeon

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fighting Whirlpool
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin, which is often mistaken for a mermaid's.

name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

name: Dragonite

abilities.name: Jet Cruise
abilities.text: Your Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.

name: Bill's Transfer
rules: Look at the top 8 cards of your deck. You may reveal any number of Pokémon you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Cycling Road
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard a Basic Energy card from their hand in order to draw a card.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

name: Grabber
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Pokémon you find there on the bottom of their deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Leftovers
rules: At the end of your turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot, heal 20 damage from it.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Caterpie

attacks.name: Leaf Munch
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Grass Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Reflective Barrier
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psyslash
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Antique Dome Fossil
rules: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.  At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

abilities.name: Domed Armor
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Antique Helix Fossil
rules: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.  At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

abilities.name: Helical Swell
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Stadium cards from their hand.

name: Energy Sticker
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Erika's Invitation
rules: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you put a Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent's Bench. If you put a Pokémon onto their Bench in this way, switch in that Pokémon to the Active Spot.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Protective Goggles
rules: The Basic Pokémon this card is attached to has no Weakness.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Rigid Band
rules: The Stage 1 Pokémon this card is attached to takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Blazing Destruction
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

name: Antique Old Amber
rules: Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.  At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

abilities.name: Amber Protection
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to this Pokémon.

name: Daisy's Help
rules: Draw 2 cards. Look at your face-down Prize cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Bulbasaur

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Free Diving
attacks.text: Put up to 3 Basic Water Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It cleverly controls its furry ears and tail to maintain its balance while swimming.

name: Zapdos ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Voltaic Float
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Lightning Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Multishot Lightning
attacks.text: This attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Moltres

abilities.name: Flare Float
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Blazing Flight
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: There are stories of this Pokémon using its radiant, flame-cloaked wings to light up paths for those lost in the mountains.

name: Dratini
flavorText: It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.

name: Mew ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Restart
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Genome Hacking
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

name: Big Air Balloon
rules: The Stage 2 Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Giovanni's Charisma
rules: Put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand. If you do, attach an Energy card from your hand to your Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Ivysaur

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Exposure to sunlight adds to its strength. Sunlight also makes the bud on its back grow larger.

name: Squirtle

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

flavorText: When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

name: Machoke

attacks.name: Mountain Ramming
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Its formidable body never gets tired. It helps people by doing work such as the moving of heavy goods.

name: Golem ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dynamic Roll
attacks.text: During your next turn, attacks used by this Pokémon do 120 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rock Blaster
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Mimic Barrier
abilities.text: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psypower
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It's known for its top-notch pantomime skills. It protects itself from all sorts of attacks by emitting auras from its fingers to create walls.

name: Omanyte

attacks.name: Tentacular Return
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a member of an ancient, extinct species. Omanyte paddles through water with its 10 tentacles, looking like it's just drifting along.

name: Arbok ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bind Down
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Menacing Fangs
attacks.text: Your opponent discards 2 cards from their hand.

name: Wigglytuff ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Expanding Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it gets +100 HP.

attacks.name: Friend Tackle
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

name: Alakazam ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dimensional Hand
attacks.text: This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is on the Bench.

name: Zapdos ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Voltaic Float
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Lightning Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Multishot Lightning
attacks.text: This attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Mew ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Restart
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Genome Hacking
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

name: Nidoking

abilities.name: Enthusiastic King
abilities.text: If you have Nidoqueen in play, ignore all Energy in the costs of attacks used by this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Venomous Impact
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Nidoking prides itself on its strength. It's forceful and spirited in battle, making use of its thick tail and diamond-crushing horn.

name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: They say that if it emits an aura from its whole body, the weather will begin to change instantly.

name: Venusaur ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Tranquil Flower
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dangerous Toxwhip
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

name: Ninetales ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heat Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Mirrored Flames
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 140 more damage.

name: Jynx ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Heart-Stopping Kiss
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, it is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Overthink
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever they flip a coin, treat it as tails.

flavorText: It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

name: Poliwhirl

attacks.name: Frog Hop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon's sweat is a slimy mucus. When captured, Poliwhirl can slither from its enemies' grasp and escape.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Tactful Tangling
attacks.text: If you played Erika's Invitation from your hand during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon's true appearance remains a mystery.

attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Bouquet Magic

attacks.name: Scratching Nails
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Skeledirge ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Vitality Song
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Burning Voice
attacks.text: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Bulbasaur
flavorText: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Reflective Barrier
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psyslash
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Squirtle

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

name: Mew ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Restart
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Genome Hacking
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

name: Greninja ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stealthy Shuriken
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Torrential Slash
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Scrappy Spark
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

name: Quaquaval ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Exciting Dance
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Spiral Shot
attacks.text: Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

name: Alakazam ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dimensional Hand
attacks.text: This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is on the Bench.

name: Kangaskhan ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Incessant Punching
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 100 damage for each heads.

name: Zapdos ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Voltaic Float
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Lightning Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Multishot Lightning
attacks.text: This attack also does 90 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Snorlax

abilities.name: Voraciousness
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put up to 2 Leftovers cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Thudding Press
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This POKéMON's stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

name: Surskit

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It secretes a thick, sweet-scented syrup from the tip of its head. It lives on weed-choked ponds.

name: Masquerain

attacks.name: Daunting Eyes
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, shuffle an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their deck.

flavorText: The antennae have distinctive patterns that look like eyes. When it rains, they grow heavy, making flight impossible.

name: Froslass ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Evanescent
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out, flip a coin. If heads, your opponent takes 1 fewer Prize card.

attacks.name: Frost Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Pansage

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It's kind enough to share them with friends.

name: Simisage

abilities.name: Monkey Trio
abilities.text: If you have Simisage, Simisear, and Simipour in play, ignore all Colorless Energy in the costs of attacks used by this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Arm Thrust Needle
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon that have an Ability, except any Simisage.

flavorText: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

name: Dwebble
flavorText: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

name: Crustle

attacks.name: Crag Bash
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks from Evolution Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They're mighty enough to crack Rhyperior's carapace.

name: Bounsweet

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its sweat is sweet, like syrup made from boiled- down fruit. Because of this, Bounsweet was highly valued in the past, when sweeteners were scarce.

name: Steenee

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Steenee spreads a sweet scent that makes others feel invigorated. This same scent is popular for antiperspirants.

name: Blipbug

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

name: Dottler

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

name: Orbeetle

attacks.name: Satellite Beam
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy card in your opponent's discard pile.

attacks.name: Brain Shake
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

name: Nymble
flavorText: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

name: Nymble
flavorText: It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.

name: Toedscool

attacks.name: Clinging Spore
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

name: Toedscool

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

name: Toedscruel

abilities.name: Slime Mold Colony
abilities.text: Cards in your opponent's discard pile can't be put into their hand by an effect of your opponent's Abilities or Trainer cards.

attacks.name: Mushroom Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.

name: Wo-Chien

attacks.name: Leaf Bringer
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Grass Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Binding Greed
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost ColorlessColorless more.

flavorText: It drains the life-force from vegetation, causing nearby forests to instantly wither and fields to turn barren.

name: Magby

attacks.name: Scorching Heater
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes its home near volcanoes. At the end of the day, Magby soaks in magma, resting and recovering from the day's fatigue.

name: Pansear

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Simisear

abilities.name: Monkey Trio
abilities.text: If you have Simisage, Simisear, and Simipour in play, ignore all Colorless Energy in the costs of attacks used by this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

name: Volcanion

attacks.name: Dual Turbo
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon and attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your discard pile to each of them.

flavorText: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

name: Fuecoco
flavorText: It lies on warm rocks and uses the heat absorbed by its square-shaped scales to create fire energy.

name: Crocalor

attacks.name: Rolling Fireball
attacks.text: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: The combination of Crocalor's fire energy and overflowing vitality has caused an egg-shaped fireball to appear on the Pokémon's head.

name: Charcadet

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

name: Charcadet

attacks.name: Fiery Fighting Spirit
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

name: Armarouge ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Crimson Armor
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes 80 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Scorching Bazooka
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Iron Moth

abilities.name: Thermal Reactor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may move any amount of Fire Energy from your other Pokémon to it.

attacks.name: Heat Ray
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Heat Ray.

flavorText: No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon's traits match up with an object described in an old book.

name: Chi-Yu

attacks.name: Flare Bringer
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Megafire of Envy
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.

name: Horsea
flavorText: They swim with dance-like motions and cause whirlpools to form. Horsea compete to see which of them can generate the biggest whirlpool.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Seadra's mouth is slender, but its suction power is strong. In an instant, Seadra can suck in food that's larger than the opening of its mouth.

name: Kingdra

attacks.name: Whirltide
attacks.text: Reveal the top 6 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy card you find there. Then, discard those Energy cards and shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Scales shed by this Pokémon have such a splendorous gleam to them that they've been given to royalty as gifts.

name: Remoraid
flavorText: Using its dorsal fin as a suction pad, it clings to a Mantine's underside to scavenge for leftovers.

name: Octillery

abilities.name: Suction Cup Draw
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Smokescreen Shot
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Drawup Power
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

name: Milotic

abilities.name: Lifeboat
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. Each player puts a Basic Pokémon from their discard pile onto their Bench. (Your opponent puts a Basic Pokémon onto their Bench first.)

attacks.name: Hypno Splash
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It's said that a glimpse of a Milotic and its beauty will calm any hostile emotions you're feeling.

name: Snorunt

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

name: Garchomp ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hydro Lander
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Sonic Dive
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Mantyke

attacks.name: Buoyant Healing
attacks.text: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It swims along with a school of Remoraid, and they'll all fight together to repel attackers.

name: Palkia

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Prize Count
attacks.text: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It has the ability to distort space. It is described as a deity in Sinnoh-region mythology.

name: Panpour

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

name: Simipour

abilities.name: Monkey Trio
abilities.text: If you have Simisage, Simisear, and Simipour in play, ignore all Colorless Energy in the costs of attacks used by this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Liquid Lashing
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

name: Vanillite
flavorText: Supposedly, this Pokémon was born from an icicle. It spews out freezing air at −58 degrees Fahrenheit to make itself more comfortable.

name: Vanillish
flavorText: It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it spares their lives afterward—it's a kind Pokémon.

name: Vanilluxe

abilities.name: Frigid Room
abilities.text: Your opponent's Pokémon that have 40 HP or less remaining can't attack.

flavorText: People believe this Pokémon formed when two Vanillish stuck together. Its body temperature is roughly 21 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Tsareena ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Icicle Sole
attacks.text: Put damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon until its remaining HP is 30.

attacks.name: Trop Kick
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.

name: Wimpod

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Wimpod gather in swarms, constantly on the lookout for danger. They scatter the moment they detect an enemy's presence.

name: Wimpod

attacks.name: Sneaky Snacking
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Wimpod gather in swarms, constantly on the lookout for danger. They scatter the moment they detect an enemy's presence.

name: Golisopod

attacks.name: Powerful Cross
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: They live in sunken ships or in holes in the seabed. When Golisopod and Grapploct battle, the loser becomes the winner's meal.

name: Golisopod ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swing and Skedaddle
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Wiglett

attacks.name: Fury Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

name: Wiglett
flavorText: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

name: Wugtrio

abilities.name: Suddenly Select
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Triple Whip
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

name: Veluza

abilities.name: Fillet Memento
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move up to 2 Water Energy cards from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.

name: Dondozo

attacks.name: Supplemental Swallow-Up
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. You may attach any number of Basic Energy cards you find there to this Pokémon. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it's bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

name: Iron Bundle

abilities.name: Hyper Blower
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.) If you do, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards.

attacks.name: Refrigerated Stream
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

name: Chien-Pao

attacks.name: Snow Bringer
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Water Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wrathful Blade
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.

name: Mewtwo ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Transfer Charge
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Psychic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Photon Kinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Elekid

attacks.name: Crackling Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's not good at storing electricity yet. This Pokémon sneaks into people's homes, looking for electrical outlets to eat electricity from.

name: Plusle

attacks.name: Plus Damage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

name: Minun

abilities.name: Buddy Pulse
abilities.text: If you have Plusle in play, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. The effect of Buddy Pulse doesn't stack.

flavorText: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

name: Blitzle

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

name: Zebstrika

attacks.name: Burst of Braying
attacks.text: Choose Basic Lightning Energy cards from your discard pile up to the number of Prize cards your opponent has taken and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
