flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

name: Joltik

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Joltik latch on to other Pokémon and suck out static electricity. They're often found sticking to Yamper's hindquarters.

name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lays traps of electrified threads near the nests of bird Pokémon, aiming to snare chicks that are not yet good at flying.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Crushing Short
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Thunder
attacks.text: This attack also does 40 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

name: Oricorio

attacks.name: Pep Up
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Volt Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The energetic, cheerful dance of these Oricorio is popular with children, but the Oricorio themselves are hard to find in Paldea.

name: Tapu Koko ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Vengeful Shock
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Extreme Current
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Toxel

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

name: Iron Hands ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Amp You Very Much
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

name: Natu

attacks.name: Triple Strike
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Because its wings aren't yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always staring at something.

name: Xatu

abilities.name: Clairvoyant Sense
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.

flavorText: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun's movement all day.

name: Latios

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

name: Deoxys

attacks.name: Genome Spiral
attacks.text: Move all Energy from this Pokémon to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

name: Yamask

attacks.name: Ominous Eyes
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: The spirit of a person from a bygone age became this Pokémon. It rambles through ruins, searching for someone who knows its face.

name: Cofagrigus ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Gold Coffin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hollow Hands
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Pumpkaboo

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

name: Gourgeist

abilities.name: Startling Pumpkin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, discard 2 random cards from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.

name: Flittle

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.

name: Flittle

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.

name: Espathra

abilities.name: Stance
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Glittering Eyes
attacks.text: If Tulip is in your discard pile, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

name: Tinkatink

attacks.name: Mountain Scrounging
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put that card into your hand. If you don't, discard that card and draw a card.

flavorText: This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it's satisfied with the result.

name: Tinkatink

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it's satisfied with the result.

name: Tinkatuff

attacks.name: Alloy Aswing
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Metal Energy attached, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: These Pokémon make their homes in piles of scrap metal. They test the strength of each other's hammers by smashing them together.

name: Tinkaton

attacks.name: Crushing Blow
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Alloyed Hammer
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Metal Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: The hammer tops 220 pounds, yet it gets swung around easily by Tinkaton as it steals whatever it pleases and carries its plunder back home.

name: Scream Tail

attacks.name: Roaring Scream
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.

name: Gimmighoul

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

name: Gimmighoul

attacks.name: Continuous Coin Toss
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

name: Iron Valiant ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Tachyon Bits
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Laser Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Hard Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

flavorText: It flies straight at its target's face, then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Toxic Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

flavorText: Its flight is soundless. It uses its lengthy tail to carry off its prey... Then its elongated fangs do the rest.

name: Groudon

attacks.name: Swelling Power
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magma Purge
attacks.text: Discard up to 4 Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

name: Gible
flavorText: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

name: Gabite

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

name: Mienfoo
flavorText: Though small, Mienfoo's temperament is fierce. Any creature that approaches Mienfoo carelessly will be greeted with a flurry of graceful attacks.

name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Three-Step Strike
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Whip Expert
attacks.text: If you attached a Pokémon Tool card from your hand to this Pokémon during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Delivered at blinding speeds, kicks from this Pokémon can shatter massive boulders into tiny pieces.

name: Hoopa ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bandit's Fist
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Bandit's Fist.

name: Minior

abilities.name: Far-Flying Meteor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, when you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gravitational Tackle
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This is its form when its shell has fallen off. The color of its core depends on the materials that made up the food it ate.

name: Toxtricity ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Knocking Hammer
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Gaia Punk
attacks.text: Discard 3 Lightning Energy from your Pokémon.

name: Nacli

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

name: Nacli

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

name: Naclstack

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon dry cures its prey by spraying salt over them. The curing process steals away the water in the prey's body.

name: Garganacl

abilities.name: Energizing Rock Salt
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

flavorText: Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

name: Klawf

attacks.name: Unhinged Scissors
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 160 more damage.

attacks.name: Boiled Press
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can't remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.

name: Flamigo

attacks.name: Combat Beak
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.

name: Slither Wing

attacks.name: Stomp Off
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Burning Turbulence
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.

name: Sandy Shocks ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Magnetic Absorption
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if your opponent has 4 or fewer Prize cards remaining, you may attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthen Spike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Ting-Lu

attacks.name: Sand Bringer
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Arrogant Impact
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has 4 or more damage counters on it, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The fear poured into an ancient ritual vessel has clad itself in rocks and dirt to become a Pokémon.

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Zubat live in caves, down where the sun's light won't reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.

name: Golbat

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.

name: Crobat

attacks.name: Echoing Madness
attacks.text: Choose Item cards or Supporter cards. During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any of the chosen cards from their hand.

flavorText: This Pokémon flaps its four wings skillfully. Crobat can fly through cramped caves without needing to slow down.

name: Absol

attacks.name: Drawareness
attacks.text: You may discard any number of cards from your hand until you have 4 or fewer. Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Enhanced Blade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Because of this Pokémon's ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.

name: Purrloin
flavorText: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

name: Liepard

attacks.name: Dishonest Swap
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This stealthy Pokémon sneaks up behind prey without making any sound at all. It competes with Thievul for territory.

name: Trubbish

attacks.name: Venomous Hit
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Chuck
attacks.text: Discard any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Venomous Hit
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dark Edge
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

name: Nickit

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

name: Thievul

abilities.name: Rob-'n'-Run
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have your opponent reveal their hand, and then you choose 2 Energy cards you find there and shuffle them into your opponent's deck.

flavorText: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

name: Morpeko

abilities.name: In a Hungry Hurry
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Energizer Wheel
attacks.text: Move 2 Darkness Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

name: Lokix

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Punishing Kick
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.

name: Brute Bonnet

abilities.name: Toxic Powder
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has an Ancient Booster Energy Capsule attached, you may make both Active Pokémon Poisoned.

attacks.name: Rampaging Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.

name: Roaring Moon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frenzied Gouging
attacks.text: Knock Out your opponent's Active Pokémon. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out in this way, this Pokémon does 200 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Calamity Storm
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

name: Jirachi

abilities.name: Stellar Veil
abilities.text: Prevent all damage counters from being placed on your Benched Pokémon by effects of attacks used by your opponent's Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Charge Energy
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.

name: Ferroseed
flavorText: Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings. Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed's spikes grow big and strong.

name: Ferrothorn

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Spinning Needles
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Spinning Needles attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Swarming Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on all of your Durant.

attacks.name: Hard Scissors
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

name: Honedge
flavorText: The blue eye on the sword's handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people's lives away.

name: Honedge

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The blue eye on the sword's handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people's lives away.

name: Doublade

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike.

flavorText: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

name: Doublade

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Slicing Blade attack does 80 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Mysterious Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex and Pokémon V.

attacks.name: Hard Bashing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

name: Aegislash ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Peerless Edge
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card you have taken.

attacks.name: Double-Edged Slash
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Zacian

attacks.name: Iron Roar
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Metal Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brave Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Able to cut down anything with a single strike, it became known as the Fairy King's Sword, and it inspired awe in friend and foe alike.

name: Skeledirge ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Incendiary Song
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may discard a Basic Fire Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. During this turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do 60 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Luster Burn
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Orthworm

attacks.name: Punch and Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Crunch-Time Rush
attacks.text: If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: When attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.

name: Gholdengo ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Coin Bonus
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw a card. If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw 1 more card.

attacks.name: Make It Rain
attacks.text: Discard any number of Basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Altaria ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Humming Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Light Pulse
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

name: Tatsugiri

attacks.name: Survival Strategy
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Data Displacement
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: In recent years, this species has been very helpful in cyberspace. These Pokémon will go around checking to make sure no suspicious data exists.

name: Porygon2

attacks.name: Powered Ball
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: After artificial intelligence was implemented in Porygon2, the Pokémon began using a strange language that only other Porygon2 understand.

name: Porygon-Z

abilities.name: Buggy Turbo
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, attach up to 4 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If tails, discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energized Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Some say an additional program made this Pokémon evolve, but even academics can't agree on whether Porygon-Z is really an evolution.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Filch
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It searches for prey from the tops of trees. When it spots its favorite food, Bounsweet, Aipom gets excited and pounces.

name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Hand Fling
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each card in your hand.

flavorText: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that's proof it really likes you.

name: Miltank

attacks.name: Lively Tackle
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon needs to be milked every day, or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk changes with the seasons.

name: Whismur

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: When Whismur cries, the sound of its own voice startles it, making the Pokémon cry even louder. It cries until it's exhausted, then it falls asleep.

name: Loudred

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The force of this Pokémon's loud voice isn't just the sound—it's also the wave of air pressure that blows opponents away and damages them.

name: Exploud

attacks.name: Sudden Shout
attacks.text: Discard your opponent's Active Pokémon and all attached cards. If this Pokémon didn't evolve from Loudred during this turn, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon can do more than just shout. To communicate with others of its kind, it'll emit all sorts of sounds from the holes in its body.

name: Spinda

abilities.name: Tangled Feet
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Confused and is damaged by an attack, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Teetering Steps
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Each Spinda's spot pattern is different. With its stumbling movements, it evades opponents' attacks brilliantly!

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Bind Wound
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It can't relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes off dirt with its wings.

name: Tandemaus

attacks.name: Damage Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

name: Tandemaus

attacks.name: Tumble Over
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

name: Maushold ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solidarity
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon for each of your Tandemaus, Maushold, and Maushold ex in play.

attacks.name: Nom-Nom-Nom Incisors
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

name: Bombirdier ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Carrier
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Shadowy Wind
attacks.text: You may put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

name: Cyclizar

attacks.name: Driving Buddy
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

name: Iron Jugulis

attacks.name: Homing Headbutt
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon that have any damage counters on them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Baryon Beam
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Future Booster Energy Capsule attached, this attack can be used for ColorlessColorlessColorless.

flavorText: It's possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

name: Ancient Booster Energy Capsule
rules: The Ancient Pokémon this card is attached to gets +60 HP, recovers from all Special Conditions, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Counter Catcher
rules: You can use this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.  Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Cursed Duster
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Defiance Vest
rules: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, the Pokémon this card is attached to takes 40 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Earthen Vessel
rules: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.  Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Future Booster Energy Capsule
rules: The Future Pokémon this card is attached to has no Retreat Cost, and the attacks it uses do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Larry
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Luxurious Cape
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn't have a Rule Box, it gets +100 HP, and if it is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, that player takes 1 more Prize card. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Mela
rules: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.    Attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Norman
rules: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Parasol Lady
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If you go second and it's your first turn, draw 8 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Professor Sada's Vitality
rules: Choose up to 2 of your Ancient Pokémon and attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of them. If you attached any Energy in this way, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Professor Turo's Scenario
rules: Put 1 of your Pokémon in play into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rika
rules: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Roark
rules: Draw 2 cards. Put a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Shauntal
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. If tails, switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Snorlax Doll
rules: If this card is in your hand when you are setting up to play, you may put it face down in the Active Spot or on your Bench as if it were a 120-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. (You can do this only when you are setting up to play.) At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.     This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. If this card is Knocked Out, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Technical Machine: Blindside
rules: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.) If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

attacks.name: Blindside
attacks.text: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Technical Machine: Devolution
rules: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.) If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

attacks.name: Devolution
attacks.text: Devolve each of your opponent's evolved Pokémon by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

name: Technical Machine: Evolution
rules: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.) If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

attacks.name: Evolution
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Technical Machine: Turbo Energize
rules: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.) If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

attacks.name: Turbo Energize
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

name: Techno Radar
rules: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.    Search your deck for up to 2 Future Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Tulip
rules: Put up to 4 in any combination of Psychic Pokémon and Basic Psychic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Medical Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

name: Crustle

attacks.name: Crag Bash
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks from Evolution Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They're mighty enough to crack Rhyperior's carapace.

name: Dottler

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

name: Toedscruel

abilities.name: Slime Mold Colony
abilities.text: Cards in your opponent's discard pile can't be put into their hand by an effect of your opponent's Abilities or Trainer cards.

attacks.name: Mushroom Drain
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It coils its 10 tentacles around prey and sucks out their nutrients, causing the prey pain. The folds along the rim of its head are a popular delicacy.

name: Magby

attacks.name: Scorching Heater
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes its home near volcanoes. At the end of the day, Magby soaks in magma, resting and recovering from the day's fatigue.

name: Iron Moth

abilities.name: Thermal Reactor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may move any amount of Fire Energy from your other Pokémon to it.

attacks.name: Heat Ray
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Heat Ray.

flavorText: No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon's traits match up with an object described in an old book.

name: Snorunt

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

name: Mantyke

attacks.name: Buoyant Healing
attacks.text: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It swims along with a school of Remoraid, and they'll all fight together to repel attackers.

name: Vanillish
flavorText: It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it spares their lives afterward—it's a kind Pokémon.

name: Wimpod

attacks.name: Sneaky Snacking
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Wimpod gather in swarms, constantly on the lookout for danger. They scatter the moment they detect an enemy's presence.

name: Veluza

abilities.name: Fillet Memento
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move up to 2 Water Energy cards from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.

name: Plusle

attacks.name: Plus Damage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out its body to create crackling noises.

name: Minun

abilities.name: Buddy Pulse
abilities.text: If you have Plusle in play, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. The effect of Buddy Pulse doesn't stack.

flavorText: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

name: Blitzle

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

name: Joltik

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Joltik latch on to other Pokémon and suck out static electricity. They're often found sticking to Yamper's hindquarters.

name: Espathra

abilities.name: Stance
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Glittering Eyes
attacks.text: If Tulip is in your discard pile, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

name: Gimmighoul

attacks.name: Continuous Coin Toss
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

name: Groudon

attacks.name: Swelling Power
attacks.text: Attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magma Purge
attacks.text: Discard up to 4 Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Three-Step Strike
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Whip Expert
attacks.text: If you attached a Pokémon Tool card from your hand to this Pokémon during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Delivered at blinding speeds, kicks from this Pokémon can shatter massive boulders into tiny pieces.

name: Minior

abilities.name: Far-Flying Meteor
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, when you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gravitational Tackle
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This is its form when its shell has fallen off. The color of its core depends on the materials that made up the food it ate.

name: Garganacl

abilities.name: Energizing Rock Salt
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.

flavorText: Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

name: Slither Wing

attacks.name: Stomp Off
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Burning Turbulence
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.

name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Chuck
attacks.text: Discard any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Venomous Hit
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Dark Edge
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

name: Morpeko

abilities.name: In a Hungry Hurry
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Energizer Wheel
attacks.text: Move 2 Darkness Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Hunger hormones affect its temperament. Until its hunger is appeased, it gets up to all manner of evil deeds.

name: Brute Bonnet

abilities.name: Toxic Powder
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has an Ancient Booster Energy Capsule attached, you may make both Active Pokémon Poisoned.

attacks.name: Rampaging Hammer
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.

name: Steelix

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

name: Ferrothorn

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Spinning Needles
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Spinning Needles attack does 100 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Mysterious Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex and Pokémon V.

attacks.name: Hard Bashing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Filch
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It searches for prey from the tops of trees. When it spots its favorite food, Bounsweet, Aipom gets excited and pounces.

name: Loudred

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The force of this Pokémon's loud voice isn't just the sound—it's also the wave of air pressure that blows opponents away and damages them.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Bind Wound
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It can't relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes off dirt with its wings.

name: Porygon-Z

abilities.name: Buggy Turbo
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, attach up to 4 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If tails, discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energized Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Some say an additional program made this Pokémon evolve, but even academics can't agree on whether Porygon-Z is really an evolution.

name: Cyclizar

attacks.name: Driving Buddy
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

name: Iron Jugulis

attacks.name: Homing Headbutt
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon that have any damage counters on them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Baryon Beam
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Future Booster Energy Capsule attached, this attack can be used for ColorlessColorlessColorless.

flavorText: It's possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

name: Froslass ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Evanescent
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out, flip a coin. If heads, your opponent takes 1 fewer Prize card.

attacks.name: Frost Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Armarouge ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Crimson Armor
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes 80 less damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Scorching Bazooka
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Garchomp ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hydro Lander
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Sonic Dive
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Tsareena ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Icicle Sole
attacks.text: Put damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon until its remaining HP is 30.

attacks.name: Trop Kick
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.

name: Golisopod ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swing and Skedaddle
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch it with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Tapu Koko ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Vengeful Shock
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 90 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Extreme Current
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Iron Hands ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Amp You Very Much
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

name: Cofagrigus ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Gold Coffin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hollow Hands
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Iron Valiant ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Tachyon Bits
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Laser Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Hoopa ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Crush
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bandit's Fist
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Bandit's Fist.

name: Toxtricity ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Knocking Hammer
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Gaia Punk
attacks.text: Discard 3 Lightning Energy from your Pokémon.

name: Sandy Shocks ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Magnetic Absorption
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if your opponent has 4 or fewer Prize cards remaining, you may attach a Basic Fighting Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthen Spike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Roaring Moon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frenzied Gouging
attacks.text: Knock Out your opponent's Active Pokémon. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out in this way, this Pokémon does 200 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Calamity Storm
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Aegislash ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Peerless Edge
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card you have taken.

attacks.name: Double-Edged Slash
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Gholdengo ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Coin Bonus
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may draw a card. If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, draw 1 more card.

attacks.name: Make It Rain
attacks.text: Discard any number of Basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

name: Altaria ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Humming Heal
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Light Pulse
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

name: Maushold ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Solidarity
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon for each of your Tandemaus, Maushold, and Maushold ex in play.

attacks.name: Nom-Nom-Nom Incisors
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

name: Bombirdier ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Carrier
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Shadowy Wind
attacks.text: You may put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

name: Larry
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Mela
rules: You can use this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.    Attach a Basic Fire Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Norman
rules: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, draw 2 more cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Parasol Lady
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If you go second and it's your first turn, draw 8 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Professor Sada's Vitality
rules: Choose up to 2 of your Ancient Pokémon and attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of them. If you attached any Energy in this way, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

rules: Put 1 of your Pokémon in play into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Professor Sada's Vitality
rules: Choose up to 2 of your Ancient Pokémon and attach a Basic Energy card from your discard pile to each of them. If you attached any Energy in this way, draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Professor Turo's Scenario
rules: Put 1 of your Pokémon in play into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Rika
rules: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put 2 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards and put them on the bottom of your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Tulip
rules: Put up to 4 in any combination of Psychic Pokémon and Basic Psychic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Garchomp ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hydro Lander
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Sonic Dive
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Iron Valiant ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Tachyon Bits
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Laser Blade
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Roaring Moon ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frenzied Gouging
attacks.text: Knock Out your opponent's Active Pokémon. If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out in this way, this Pokémon does 200 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Calamity Storm
attacks.text: You may discard a Stadium in play. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.

name: Beach Court
rules: The Retreat Cost of each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is Colorless less.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

name: Counter Catcher
rules: You can use this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.  Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Luxurious Cape
rules: If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn't have a Rule Box, it gets +100 HP, and if it is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, that player takes 1 more Prize card. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

name: Reversal Energy
rules: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.    If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, and if this card is attached to an Evolution Pokémon that doesn't have a Rule Box (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes), this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 3 Energy at a time.

name: Pineco
flavorText: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

name: Forretress ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Exploding Energy
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 5 Basic Grass Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: These Pokémon's bodies are constantly burning. Magmar are feared as one of the causes behind fires.

name: Maractus

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They're jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.

name: Magmortar

attacks.name: Volcanic Heat
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Living in the crater of a volcano has caused this Pokémon's body to resemble its environment— it has an organ similar to a magma chamber.

name: Heat Rotom

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Into the Deep
attacks.text: Put up to 3 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Freeze-Dry
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: ﻿When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

name: Camerupt

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Cinder Cannon
attacks.text: You may discard a Fighting Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in the crater of a volcano. It is well known that the humps on its back erupt every 10 years.

name: Charcadet
flavorText: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

name: Kilowattrel

attacks.name: United Thunder
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: These Pokémon get nervous when they're not in a group of six. The minute even one member of the group goes missing, Exeggcute become cowardly.

name: Natu

attacks.name: Triple Strike
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Because its wings aren't yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always staring at something.

name: Toedscool

attacks.name: Furious Kicks
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

name: Toedscruel ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Protective Mycelium
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)

attacks.name: Colony Rush
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached.

name: Espathra ex
rules: Tera: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).
Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dazzling Gaze
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon cost Colorless more.

attacks.name: Psy Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Blazing Destruction
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

flavorText: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

name: Frigibax

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: Frigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.

name: Raichu

abilities.name: Electrical Grounding
abilities.text: When 1 of your Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, you may move a Lightning Energy from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

name: Chinchou

attacks.name: Shine On
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put that card on the bottom of your deck.

flavorText: On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.

name: Charmeleon

abilities.name: Flare Veil
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)

flavorText: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

name: Numel

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Magma of almost 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit courses through its body. When it grows cold, the magma hardens and slows it.

name: Armarouge

abilities.name: Fire Off
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Fire Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flame Cannon
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Aqua Spark
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Water Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Psychic Loop
attacks.text: Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: When they work together, Exeggutor's three heads can put out powerful psychic energy. Cloudy days make this Pokémon sluggish.

name: Xatu

abilities.name: Clairvoyant Sense
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic Psychic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.

flavorText: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun's movement all day.

name: Gardevoir ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Psychic Embrace
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Psychic Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can't use this Ability on a Pokémon that would be Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Miracle Force
attacks.text: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

name: Chimecho

attacks.name: Sleep Inducer
attacks.text: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

name: Houndstone

attacks.name: Last Respects
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Psychic Pokémon in your discard pile.

flavorText: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn't like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

name: Annihilape

attacks.name: Rage Fist
attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

attacks.name: Dynamite Punch
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

name: Scraggy
flavorText: It protects itself with its durable skin. It's thought that this Pokémon will evolve once its skin has completely stretched out.

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.

name: Lechonk

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.

name: Electric Generator
rules: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards you find there to your Benched Lightning Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Attraction Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. Put 6 damage counters instead of 3 on that Pokémon for this Special Condition.

flavorText: The auspicious shape of this Pokémon's nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.

name: Nest Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.

name: Paldean Student
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn't have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. For each Paldean Student card (not including this card) in your discard pile, you may search for an additional Pokémon in this way. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Fidough
flavorText: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough's breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon's vicinity.

name: Paldean Student
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn't have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. For each Paldean Student card (not including this card) in your discard pile, you may search for an additional Pokémon in this way. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Dachsbun

abilities.name: Well-Baked Body
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't be Burned. Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Fire Pokémon.

flavorText: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon's body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

name: Technical Machine: Crisis Punch
rules: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.) If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

attacks.name: Crisis Punch
attacks.text: You can use this attack only when your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining.

name: Flittle

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.

name: Ultra Ball
rules: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.
Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Gimmighoul

attacks.name: Chest-ouflage
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

name: Barboach

attacks.name: Hide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey's location.

name: Pineco
flavorText: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

name: Clobbopus

attacks.name: Feint
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn't alarmed when that happens—it knows they'll grow back. It's about as smart as a three-year-old.

name: Snover
flavorText: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain's lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

name: Grapploct
